core_mechanism: grand-canonical reservoir coupling: a spatial subregion is held in equilibrium with an external particle reservoir at fixed chemical potential, so particles are freely inserted/removed and exchanged across the boundary between regions described at different levels
brief_reason: The abstract recasts adaptive resolution as an "effective generalized grand canonical approach" that permits "free exchange of molecules between open regions of different resolution," i.e. open-boundary particle exchange with a reservoir at fixed chemical potential.
